title: Iraq at the 2026 World Cup: roster, list, players and group stage schedule
original_text: Iraq reached the World Cup through the playoff route, but belief is soaring across the Middle Eastern nation as it chases a long-awaited return to soccer’s biggest tournament. After making its only World Cup appearance in 1986, Iraq is back in the spotlight with a monumental challenge ahead. The team, coached by Spaniard Jesús Casas, must find a way to compete with some of the sport’s biggest stars, including those representing powerhouse nations such as France and Norway. World Cup 2026 calculator: simulate groups and matchups and generate your World Cup bracket prediction Jesús Casas Iraq appears to have drawn one of the most difficult groups at the 2026 World Cup. The Lions of Mesopotamia will kick off their campaign against a talented Norway squad before facing France and Senegal, two more major tests, as they look to pull off a surprise and reach the knockout stage.
